Topic: Linear Inequalities and Absolute Value Inequalities
Problem Statement: For the following exercises, graph as described.
Question: Graph both straight lines (left-hand side being y1 and right-hand side being y2) on the same axes. Find the point of intersection and solve the inequality by observing where it is true comparing the y -values of the lines. See the interval where the inequality is true. $$x + 3 < 3 x - 4$$
Answer: Where the blue is below the orange line; point of intersection is $$x = 3.5.$$ $$( 3.5 , \infty )$$
